title: Character Display Attributes
text: Character Display Attributes Each character in Fonts 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, and 7 can be formatted using bold, italic, or underline. The collective term for this kind of formatting is a ‘shape’. It applies to text formatting of data, primarily on printers. By combining each of these attributes, each character in a font can appear in the following different shapes: Normal Underline Italics Bold Bold and italics Italics and underline Bold and underline Bold, italics, and underline IBM Fonts 0-7 and DEC Fonts 0-1 For these character sets, each character is preceded by two bytes to indicate the desired character display attribute. The hexadecimal values for these bytes are shown in the table: (bol=bold, ita=italic, und=underline) Table 1. Storing character display attributes for IBM Fonts 0-7 and DEC Fonts 0-1 und ita und/ita bol und/bol ita/bol und/ita/bol 19.28 19.30 19.38 19.40 19.48 19.50 19.58 DEC Fonts 2-7 For these character sets, the leading two bytes of each character are replaced by the two bytes shown in the following table (in hexadecimal): (bol=bold, ita=italic, und=underline) Table 2. Storing character display attributes for DEC Fonts 2-7 Fonts Replace these bytes With these bytes for character attributes und ita und/ita bol und/bol ita/bol und/ita/bol 2-3 19.22 19.2A 19.32 19.3A 19.42 19.4A 19.52 19.5A 4-5 19.24 19.2C 19.34 19.3C 19.44 19.4C 19.54 19.5C 6-7 19.26 19.2E 19.36 19.3E 19.46 19.4E 19.56 19.5E For example, the subscript 0 ( 0 ) in Font 3 for DEC is 19.22.B0. When the bold display attribute is applied, this becomes 19.42.B0. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
